title: prunus communis subsp. divaricata
intro: Prunus communis subsp. divaricata, also known as the wild cherry or European bird cherry, is a species of flowering plant in the cherry genus Prunus. The medicinal properties of Prunus communis subsp. divaricata include its use as an anti-inflammatory and antioxidant agent, which can help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, and its health benefits include its ability to lower cholesterol levels and improve cardiovascular health. Prunus communis subsp. divaricata can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusions, and is also used in the form of cherry kernel oil, which is rich in antioxidants and has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties.
